engines_involved.method: record_cost(org_id, incident_id, incident_name, incident_type, cost_category, amount, description)
engines_involved.role: primary — initialises a $0 cost tracking record for every new incident
engines_involved.status: IMPLEMENTED (incident_cost_engine.py confirmed on disk, class IncidentCostEngine at line 66, record_cost at line 162)

trigger_sources: on_alert_created() when it calls IncidentOrchestrationEngine.create_incident() and that engine emits INCIDENT_CREATED
on_threat_detected() when severity is critical/high and IncidentOrchestrationEngine emits INCIDENT_CREATED
Manual incident creation via POST /api/v1/incident-orchestration if the router emits INCIDENT_CREATED

registration.legacy_event_type: NOT REGISTERED — INCIDENT_CREATED absent from legacy EventBus type_map

current_state.summary: Single-engine flow. IncidentCostEngine.record_cost() is called unconditionally for every INCIDENT_CREATED event. The purpose is to initialise a zero-dollar cost tracking entry so the incident has a cost record from the moment it is created. incident_type is hardcoded 'other', cost_category is hardcoded 'investigation', amount is hardcoded 0.0.
current_state.entity_id_as_incident_id: entity_id from the event payload is used as incident_id. If entity_id is empty string (default), IncidentCostEngine will store cost with incident_id='' which creates a data integrity problem.

what_works: IncidentCostEngine.record_cost() is correctly called with org_id, incident_id (from entity_id), incident_name (from title), and zero-dollar initial amount.
The initialisation pattern correctly establishes cost tracking from incident birth rather than requiring manual cost entry later.
_safe_call isolation: cost tracking failure does not affect incident lifecycle.
what_doesnt: INCIDENT_CREATED is missing from the legacy EventBus type_map — legacy-bus emitters bypass this subscriber.
entity_id defaults to empty string '' if not provided — IncidentCostEngine.record_cost() will store a cost entry with incident_id='' creating an invalid record.
incident_type is hardcoded 'other' — IncidentCostEngine supports 10 cost categories and multiple incident types; the actual incident type is never forwarded.
IncidentOrchestrationEngine.create_incident() does not appear to emit INCIDENT_CREATED on the event bus after storing, meaning this subscriber may never fire in practice for auto-created incidents.
No downstream chain after cost initialisation — IRPlaybookEngine, SLAEngine, and IncidentMetricsEngine are not triggered.
No INCIDENT_CREATED emission check — the circular dependency between THREAT_DETECTED → incident → INCIDENT_CREATED → cost is untested.
The $0 placeholder cost record has no flag distinguishing it from a real $0-cost investigation, which pollutes cost analytics.
acceptance_criteria: AC1: Given INCIDENT_CREATED with entity_id='inc-123' and title='Ransomware attack', IncidentCostEngine.record_cost() is called with incident_id='inc-123', incident_name='Ransomware attack', amount=0.0, cost_category='investigation'.
AC2: Given INCIDENT_CREATED with entity_id='' (empty), the subscriber must log a WARNING and skip the IncidentCostEngine call rather than storing an invalid record.
AC3: INCIDENT_CREATED must be added to the legacy EventBus type_map.
AC4: IncidentOrchestrationEngine.create_incident() must emit INCIDENT_CREATED on the TrustGraph bus after successfully persisting the incident, carrying org_id, entity_id=incident_id, and title.
AC5: The zero-dollar cost record must be marked with a flag (e.g. is_placeholder=true) so cost analytics can exclude it from financial summaries.
AC6: incident_type from the event payload must be forwarded to IncidentCostEngine if present, falling back to 'other' only when absent.
AC7: After cost initialisation, an INCIDENT_TRIAGED event (or equivalent signal) must be emitted so SLAEngine and IRPlaybookEngine can start their timers.
AC8: A pytest test verifies the full chain: INCIDENT_CREATED event → IncidentCostEngine called with correct args, and that empty entity_id is rejected.
